In just two months, Nikki has held 33 political events all across the country, proving once again that no one will outwork Nikki Haley. This week, Nikki wrapped up her latest Iowa swing where she stopped in six cities, met with hundreds of voters and dozens of farmers, launched the Women for Nikki coalition, and made friends with the cutest little piglet. Nikki is headed back to New Hampshire at the end of this month to host multiple town halls in Bedford, Henniker, and Laconia. Take a look at this week's highlights. IOWA RECAP: " alias="WATCH NOW. >>" conversion="false" data-linkto="https://">WATCH NOW. >> Day One – Salix, IA Town Hall Sioux City Journal: “At Salix campaign event, Haley addresses China, national debt, education” ( Sioux City Journal, 4/12/23) KCAU: “GOP presidential candidate Nikki Haley is currently meeting with voters in Salix. A large crowd started gathering more than an hour before the former Ambassador to the United Nations arrived.” ( KCAU , 4/10/23) Rep. Randy Feenstra: “Beating Joe Biden starts with the #IaCaucus! I’m excited to have @NikkiHaley with me this week traveling #IA04 where Faith, Family and Conservative Values make our state strong!” ( Twitter , 4/10/23) Day Two – Denison, Storm Lake, Newell, and Fort Dodge KCCI: “She [Haley] plans to set herself apart from other republicans with a grassroots campaign. ‘No one will outwork me in this race, no one. No one will outsmart me in this race. I will tell you, you’re not going to see me come in, do a rally and leave. You’re going to see me here over and over and over again.’ Haley visited Denison, Storm Lake Newell, and Fort Dodge this week.” ( KCCI , 4/12/23) The Messenger: “Haley is the first presidential hopeful to visit Fort Dodge in advance of the 2024 caucuses.” ( The Messenger, 4/12/23) Sen. Joni Ernst: “Great to be with my friends @RandyFeenstra and @NikkiHaley! Iowans in Denison are fired up for the 2024 GOP #iacaucus!” ( Twitter , 4/11/23) Rep. Randy Feenstra: “In Iowa, we like our pork on grills and at picnics. In Congress, Democrats like their pork funneled to liberal priorities and coastal elites. Our country needs MORE #IA04 pork and LESS liberal pork bankrupting our children and grandchildren. @NikkiHaley agrees!” ( Twitter , 4/11/23) Iowa GOP Chairman Jeff Kaufmann: “Great crowd at our @IowaGOP Fort Dodge reception yesterday evening with Amb. @NikkiHaley and Rep. @RandyFeenstra. Iowa is ready for change from the disastrous Biden presidency.” ( Twitter , 4/12/23) RealClearPolitics’ Philip Wegmann: “Iowa GOP chair @kaufmannGOP asks @NikkiHaley to fill in the blank: ‘As people leave tonight, and they think to themselves, you know, Nikki Haley is a…’Haley replies, ‘she’s a badass.’ Crowd laughs and applauds.” ( Twitter , 4/11/23) NBC News’ Ali Vitali: “She’s an interesting candidate for a lot of reasons, but specifically for the profile HALEY cuts on foreign policy/national security. Lots of weaving between domestic fights from her time as SC gov to foreign policy on China, Iran, Russia. Her expansive resume really shows here.” ( Twitter , 4/11/23) Day Three – Des Moines, IA Women for Nikki Radio Iowa: “‘We are going to touch every hand. We are going to take every question. We’re not taking any shortcuts because we’re going to do it the right way because you deserve that.’” ( Radio Iowa , 4/12/23) Rep. Zach Nunn: “Hey @donlemon, still sticking with this woman is past her prime? @NikkiHaley was on 🔥 today in Des Moines! Our April mid-month deadline is at midnight tonight, and I am asking for your early investment in our 2024 Republican campaigns.

As bears emerge from their dens this spring after hibernating for winter, they have no idea that they might not make it to summertime. This spring, blooming flowers could be splattered by the blood of mama bears being gunned down while looking for food to provide for their babies -- **leaving cubs orphaned and alone. [Friend, we can't leave these helpless cubs to fend for themselves. Help protect mama bears, their babies, and the planet: donate $27 to help us reach our $3,650 goal before it's too late.](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1723266?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)**
Trophy hunters are tracking down and targeting bears across the U.S., putting them more at risk each day. Mama bears are being torn apart by bullets, leaving their babies to starve, freeze, or fall prey to other species. But private interests are working to remove grizzly bear protections so they can kill more bears and take over the lands they call home -- all to turn a profit.
Now, fewer than 2,000 grizzlies remain in the lower 48 states -- and trophy hunters show no sign of stopping the slaughter. In fact, they are chomping at the bit to hunt grizzlies to the point where they are preemptively drafting hunting plans for these vulnerable creatures. We can't stand by and watch while they chip away at protections for grizzlies, opening up the lands they call home for the sake of mining, logging, and factory farming.
**Don't let any more baby bears lose their mamas to ruthless trophy hunting and protect our planet: Donate $27 by midnight TONIGHT to help us reach our $3,650 goal.**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
[Donate $27](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1723262?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)

[Donate another amount](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1723265?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
At their peak, grizzlies in the continental U.S. numbered at 50,000! But by 1975, reckless human activity, like hunting, trapping, and habitat loss, decimated their numbers -- prompting their Endangered Species Act protection.
Today, faced with shrinking habitat, the loss of critical food sources, and many impacts of climate change, grizzly bears are struggling to survive. Their populations outside Alaska have plummeted to a mere 2% of their historic population. Fewer and fewer cubs are surviving to reach adulthood, and adult bears are dying more quickly because of rapid changes -- from a shifting food supply to being forced to hibernate later and later in the fall.
Now, states in the northern Rockies are working to remove ESA protections for threatened grizzly bears.
Stripping grizzlies of their ESA protections would be devastating -- just last year, Idaho Fish and Game found a female grizzly with 12 BULLETS in her. She was shot an estimated 40 TIMES. This is the level of ferocity grizzly bears are facing without strengthened ESA protections.
Black bears are already facing the horrors that could await grizzlies if private interests succeed. Trophy hunters wait until they leave the safety of their dens to trap them with painful metal clamps or release hunting dogs to track and terrorize bears before they are murdered. No animal deserves this brutal fate.
**Act now to preserve and strengthen ESA protections for grizzlies and protect our planet before it's too late. Make your $27 contribution today.**

---
A top predator, grizzlies play a pivotal role in keeping our ecosystem healthy and balanced. Standing up to eight feet tall and weighing up to 800 pounds, these intelligent omnivores enhance biodiversity, enrich soils, regulate prey populations, and transport nutrients through different ecosystems as they roam far and wide. Scientists are continuously discovering more and more ways grizzlies play a pivotal role in the ecosystems they inhabit.
Endangered species laws provide critical protection for America's iconic animal species. They are also some of our most effective safeguards in the fight to protect our public lands from Big Agriculture, fossil fuel, and factory farm industries, and curb catastrophic climate change.
For the sake of our wildlife and our planet, we can't let Big Polluters and their cronies in Congress destroy our public lands and wipe out our endangered species.
Your membership support to Friends of the Earth today will allow us to take a stand against private interests who want to hunt down vulnerable species like bears, wreak havoc on their Endangered Species Act protections, and contribute to our planet's destruction.
The inhumane treatment of bears and their cubs cannot continue. One bullet will not only kill mother bears, but the slaughter means that her cubs will be left to die as well. Please, Friend, help us put a stop to the deaths of America's most iconic species.
**Friend, help save grizzlies from an unimaginable fate. Donate $27 now to help us reach our $3,650 goal by midnight tonight.**

clean_body: | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Dear Friend, | Protect grizzlies from vicious trophy hunting and Big Industry attacks. Donate $27 to help us reach our $3,650 goal before it’s too late. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | == == == == As bears emerge from their dens this spring after hibernating for winter, they have no idea that they might not make it to summertime. This spring, blooming flowers could be splattered by the blood of mama bears being gunned down while looking for food to provide for their babies -- leaving cubs orphaned and alone. == Trophy hunters are tracking down and targeting bears across the U.S., putting them more at risk each day. Mama bears are being torn apart by bullets, leaving their babies to starve, freeze, or fall prey to other species. But private interests are working to remove grizzly bear protections so they can kill more bears and take over the lands they call home -- all to turn a profit. Now, fewer than 2,000 grizzlies remain in the lower 48 states -- and trophy hunters show no sign of stopping the slaughter. In fact, they are chomping at the bit to hunt grizzlies to the point where they are preemptively drafting hunting plans for these vulnerable creatures. We can't stand by and watch while they chip away at protections for grizzlies, opening up the lands they call home for the sake of mining, logging, and factory farming. Don't let any more baby bears lose their mamas to ruthless trophy hunting and protect our planet: Donate $27 by midnight TONIGHT to help us reach our $3,650 goal. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: == == == == At their peak, grizzlies in the continental U.S. numbered at 50,000! But by 1975, reckless human activity, like hunting, trapping, and habitat loss, decimated their numbers -- prompting their Endangered Species Act protection. Today, faced with shrinking habitat, the loss of critical food sources, and many impacts of climate change, grizzly bears are struggling to survive. Their populations outside Alaska have plummeted to a mere 2% of their historic population. Fewer and fewer cubs are surviving to reach adulthood, and adult bears are dying more quickly because of rapid changes -- from a shifting food supply to being forced to hibernate later and later in the fall. Now, states in the northern Rockies are working to remove ESA protections for threatened grizzly bears. Stripping grizzlies of their ESA protections would be devastating -- just last year, Idaho Fish and Game found a female grizzly with 12 BULLETS in her. She was shot an estimated 40 TIMES. This is the level of ferocity grizzly bears are facing without strengthened ESA protections. Black bears are already facing the horrors that could await grizzlies if private interests succeed. Trophy hunters wait until they leave the safety of their dens to trap them with painful metal clamps or release hunting dogs to track and terrorize bears before they are murdered. No animal deserves this brutal fate. Act now to preserve and strengthen ESA protections for grizzlies and protect our planet before it's too late. Make your $27 contribution today. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: == == == == A top predator, grizzlies play a pivotal role in keeping our ecosystem healthy and balanced. Standing up to eight feet tall and weighing up to 800 pounds, these intelligent omnivores enhance biodiversity, enrich soils, regulate prey populations, and transport nutrients through different ecosystems as they roam far and wide. Scientists are continuously discovering more and more ways grizzlies play a pivotal role in the ecosystems they inhabit. Endangered species laws provide critical protection for America's iconic animal species. They are also some of our most effective safeguards in the fight to protect our public lands from Big Agriculture, fossil fuel, and factory farm industries, and curb catastrophic climate change. For the sake of our wildlife and our planet, we can't let Big Polluters and their cronies in Congress destroy our public lands and wipe out our endangered species. Your membership support to Friends of the Earth today will allow us to take a stand against private interests who want to hunt down vulnerable species like bears, wreak havoc on their Endangered Species Act protections, and contribute to our planet's destruction. The inhumane treatment of bears and their cubs cannot continue. One bullet will not only kill mother bears, but the slaughter means that her cubs will be left to die as well. Please, Friend, help us put a stop to the deaths of America's most iconic species. Friend, help save grizzlies from an unimaginable fate. Donate $27 now to help us reach our $3,650 goal by midnight tonight. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: == == == == Standing with you, Raena Garcia Fossil fuel and lands campaigner, Friends of the Earth | Contact Us: Friends of the Earth U.S. Washington, D.C. | Berkeley, CA 1-877-843-8687

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

clean_body: The number of grassroots donors will likely be one of the main criteria for an invitation to the first Presidential Debate. | Fellow American, Yesterday, the RNC announced the location of the first Presidential Debate. I am going to put in the work EVERY DAY to make sure I am on that debate stage. While we don't know today the criteria to be invited to the main debate stage yet, it's clear that the number of unique grassroots donors will play a major role. To make sure I can share my vision for America on the debate stage, | Whatever amount you are comfortable giving will help us drive forward and get our ideas a prominent spot on the debate stage. Thanks, Vivek Ramaswamy GET TO KNOW VIVEK: Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam , along with his second book, Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence. He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution. Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved. In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics. | | | ---|---|--- | Paid for by Vivek 2024 Contributions to Vivek 2024 are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. Funds received in response to this solicitation are subject to federal contribution limits. Contributions from corporations, labor unions, foreign nationals without green cards, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer for individuals whose contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle. Defend the Constitution 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 |

Our friends at _Tactical USA_ had some extras of these Dash Cams in their warehouse...

name: Corey DeAngelis, Senior Fellow AFC
subject: How we break the teachers unions’ monopoly
disclaimer_text: Paid for by American Federation for Children
body:
These unions are more interested in protecting the status quo and serving their own interests than helping students succeed.

| | |
---
| | [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=1f7235a2d88349198aa0c25437402724&_e=39scvT0Nn1rKu8gNbqz16tlPed2ElD6vC5YROUkcKoy1jXy0urAn1zHCVIXqvWEvgh464K6EfNitjWV80BfH5avMCY6Qaenx4nD6rIXgqUU7TQuKOM66qwri0xZRFoCLak7sE4JJQuqusvEirIcw6ZJOryg_YH722Wj9FxJTkXDnbQJG5i_7enJ2oknklQHUDowI3Bu5lnvnnzn-jQb6X7ij9vbugHYG6Om6CLElQgAdDp_HgTtRIamUhQ6aKoilanrMcA0gGVruxW61YHXOOODTiKEeHxKWQjGo-COCLgmdg7k6qjOV3URTdnaDEk0Qzw1WP9CUSRQQ8qyIiqnjRl6giYVrSqOQ-pSMxNT4-a8%3D)
---
| | Hi Friend,
I recently spoke with Fox News about the problem with teachers unions, and I didn't hold back. Power-hungry teachers unions consistently fight to trap kids in the failing government schools they staff. Their opposition to parental rights in education has everything to do with maintaining their control over the minds of other people's children. It's all about power. Teachers unions are more interested in protecting the status quo and serving their own interests than helping students succeed. Enough is enough. They've overplayed their hand and now we are freeing families from the clutches of the teachers unions once and for all. At the American Federation for Children, we're taking power over education away from teachers unions and putting it back into the hands of parents one state at a time. Already this year, we've helped deliver HUGE wins in Iowa, Utah, Arkansas, and Florida. [_**Friend, I 'm asking you to join the most important fight of our generation by pledging a donation of any amount. Can we count on your support today?**_](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=1f7235a2d88349198aa0c25437402724&_e=39scvT0Nn1rKu8gNbqz16tlPed2ElD6vC5YROUkcKoy1jXy0urAn1zHCVIXqvWEvgh464K6EfNitjWV80BfH5avMCY6Qaenx4nD6rIXgqUU7TQuKOM66qwri0xZRFoCLak7sE4JJQuqusvEirIcw6ZJOryg_YH722Wj9FxJTkXDnbQJG5i_7enJ2oknklQHUDowI3Bu5lnvnnzn-jQb6X5PurWq6lCynyNmeDE__15x94p7O86NUTdRo_retCVhd8ptVVU7xzDbnYLhjrdeYlICGNJhwysuxMvSf5FCaNbG5KfvKPM-74Lm1P3s3e5Y_v1rSpg-L9ZudtujyAeJ-fpCyEefXqATQp4NCrfO0XAc%3D)

clean_body: These unions are more interested in protecting the status quo and serving their own interests than helping students succeed. | | | | | | | Hi Friend, I recently spoke with Fox News about the problem with teachers unions, and I didn't hold back. Power-hungry teachers unions consistently fight to trap kids in the failing government schools they staff. Their opposition to parental rights in education has everything to do with maintaining their control over the minds of other people's children. It's all about power. Teachers unions are more interested in protecting the status quo and serving their own interests than helping students succeed. Enough is enough. They've overplayed their hand and now we are freeing families from the clutches of the teachers unions once and for all. At the American Federation for Children, we're taking power over education away from teachers unions and putting it back into the hands of parents one state at a time. Already this year, we've helped deliver HUGE wins in Iowa, Utah, Arkansas, and Florida. | | | | | | | | | | Your contribution will help ensure every family has access to quality education options and every child can reach their full potential. name: Candace Owens, Co-founder of BLEXIT
subject: The most personal email I’ve ever sent you
body:
But I must share my story and tell you why I created the BLEXIT Foundation.

[](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=163784322b4e46c1ad09ea60c3b2a74d&_e=F2uWvgIS7XhJSfOP79NdJjYQxbM7P3RlwtaB3Lk4yoPx8tyLI3c3fpYlmR0S4pAIgJsN4GcmIjuez-K52Z_hZyIWitsXYEWV3J5zDOfrQj9Roj-DfFIfeUWXX7BNDV8qvJWpbxRA5N3glTnssVWMiYWpiyiuU-rWRHs55Xbd14VNu6Zdoox_RvSZA9jEfHTQlfWrg6m4-K4PSpBgu4E5P7u84GjEb5MGZuMZktb8W_HfqaZoUKs-8F9XBinVnmAVlkSrX1I1n2Z7gzFXQxGAr4R8Bfq7iwirGzRH2RdI6xE%3D)

Friend,

This is the most personal and vulnerable email I have ever sent you... But I must share my story and tell you why I created the BLEXIT Foundation.

Before I share my story, allow me to introduce the BLEXIT Foundation.

**For too long, I have watched Black Americans force-fed a message of victimhood, fear, defeat, and dependence. And, this has to stop.** In 2019, with Brandon Tatum, we created BLEXIT Foundation - a nonprofit organization - dedicated to bringing traditional American principles and a message of empowerment to urban minority communities.

Our message to America's black and minority communities is that you are not a victim. We promote economic independence and individual freedom, strengthening the nuclear family, and fostering a deeper appreciation for patriotism in Black American culture.

[_**Please support the BLEXIT Foundation 's mission today by helping me reach our goal of raising $25,000 from new online donors. You can click here to donate.**_](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=163784322b4e46c1ad09ea60c3b2a74d&_e=F2uWvgIS7XhJSfOP79NdJjYQxbM7P3RlwtaB3Lk4yoPx8tyLI3c3fpYlmR0S4pAIgJsN4GcmIjuez-K52Z_hZyIWitsXYEWV3J5zDOfrQj9Roj-DfFIfeUWXX7BNDV8qvJWpbxRA5N3glTnssVWMiV4rWonIjwRf0wc8Gc7QnPgH8Q9jiUnTGQBUjYdNW8f-CIotpNcSSMb0mwUQ5gcDf_tJSIzmNNrlQZX7Za0VwlZT0vplJ9maHBg-4KgX760tL2Up9y3P8EQLVkgUjsP_xVaKMe3HSNm8GRLlmrj5KVg%3D)

**_Now I 'll share my story_... racism, death threats, and empowering black communities.**

Growing up as a black woman, I can recall learning how "helpless" I was told I was. And this message - which the media forces Black Americans to listen to - is heartbreaking.

We're taught to believe we're victims, that nothing will ever change, the police are our enemies, and racism prevents us from ever achieving our goals.

This is WRONG.

I've dealt with racism... _**I 've even received death threats**_. Racism is an ugly form of collectivism and is unacceptable.

But the solution is NOT to become a victim and give up.

I created the BLEXIT Foundation to empower black communities, minorities, and the people in my life whom I love. Since our start in 2019, BLEXIT has grown substantially with over 41 grassroots chapter programs in 41 states, over 14,000 volunteer members and local community leaders, and nearly 60,000 grassroots supporters.

[_**Friend, I am inviting you to support the BLEXIT Foundation and help us spread our winning message of entrepreneurship, school choice, faith, and family. My goal is to raise $25,000 from online supporters to fund our mission. Please click here to donate $10, $50, $100, $250, $500, $1,000 or any amount. **_](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=163784322b4e46c1ad09ea60c3b2a74d&_e=F2uWvgIS7XhJSfOP79NdJjYQxbM7P3RlwtaB3Lk4yoPx8tyLI3c3fpYlmR0S4pAIgJsN4GcmIjuez-K52Z_hZyIWitsXYEWV3J5zDOfrQj9Roj-DfFIfeUWXX7BNDV8qvJWpbxRA5N3glTnssVWMidBqBDXDIXHEOVMaLxtwiMhAORFvCwBzCUqZfRFNIMxfoS6LUBqy4q8C0mTyqN2cVXRIN5IvVwHtQ-JghEVSuh7F1hFsNj7A8wHjwKuPUGSv3ODB4EGjdC2jloKg8-MolS83wI-u5i7vt078uSPxBGE%3D)

I am breaking the rules.

It seems like every email I receive in my inbox engages in fear-mongering, makes big promises, and demands you and I make a donation without any of the details... _I 'm not doing that_.

You should know the details, believe in our mission, and understand how BLEXIT is changing the direction of our urban communities and our country.

So I am taking a risk by sending you a long email with all the details to empower YOU to choose to support the BLEXIT Foundation.**Please read to the end of this email (I have an important message at the end you don 't want to miss).**

When I founded BLEXIT, I wanted to make sure above all else we were NOT just a "think tank", but rather we pride ourselves on being a thoughtful and quick acting "DO-tank". We're doing things no other organization in the country is doing.

Here are the BLEXIT Foundation's values, what we're doing to reach Black communities, and what we're fighting for:

* **School Choice:** Often, the people allegedly "fighting for minorities" are doing everything in their power to keep Black and Hispanic students locked into awful schools.

The ugly truth is that these schools are simply brainwashing centers attacking the nuclear family, creating victims, and teaching their twisted values. BLEXIT is fighting for School Choice to empower young Black and Hispanic students to learn real skills, have more opportunities, and escape poverty. * **Criminal Justice Reform:** The mainstream media wants urban communities to hate our law enforcement officers. This ugly message is setting Black Americans back. The BLEXIT Foundation supports our men and women who serve as police officers and defend our communities.

Yes, we do need criminal justice reform. But we believe cooperating with our police officers and connecting as a community is critical to creating justice. BLEXIT Foundation is starting conversations with police officers and urban communities. The media wants you to believe cops are evil! This is wrong. But people never hear this message.

The BLEXIT Foundation is going places where no one else goes. We're hosting events in Detroit, Baltimore, and Tampa and starting real conversations. We're not just preaching to the choir, and these aren't boring events. They have music, art, and real conversations, and they are fun! clean_body: But I must share my story and tell you why I created the BLEXIT Foundation. Friend, This is the most personal and vulnerable email I have ever sent you... But I must share my story and tell you why I created the BLEXIT Foundation. Before I share my story, allow me to introduce the BLEXIT Foundation. For too long, I have watched Black Americans force-fed a message of victimhood, fear, defeat, and dependence. And, this has to stop. In 2019, with Brandon Tatum, we created BLEXIT Foundation - a nonprofit organization - dedicated to bringing traditional American principles and a message of empowerment to urban minority communities. Our message to America's black and minority communities is that you are not a victim. We promote economic independence and individual freedom, strengthening the nuclear family, and fostering a deeper appreciation for patriotism in Black American culture. Now I 'll share my story... racism, death threats, and empowering black communities. Growing up as a black woman, I can recall learning how "helpless" I was told I was. And this message - which the media forces Black Americans to listen to - is heartbreaking. We're taught to believe we're victims, that nothing will ever change, the police are our enemies, and racism prevents us from ever achieving our goals. This is WRONG. I've dealt with racism... I 've even received death threats. Racism is an ugly form of collectivism and is unacceptable. But the solution is NOT to become a victim and give up. I created the BLEXIT Foundation to empower black communities, minorities, and the people in my life whom I love. Since our start in 2019, BLEXIT has grown substantially with over 41 grassroots chapter programs in 41 states, over 14,000 volunteer members and local community leaders, and nearly 60,000 grassroots supporters. I am breaking the rules. It seems like every email I receive in my inbox engages in fear-mongering, makes big promises, and demands you and I make a donation without any of the details... I 'm not doing that. You should know the details, believe in our mission, and understand how BLEXIT is changing the direction of our urban communities and our country. So I am taking a risk by sending you a long email with all the details to empower YOU to choose to support the BLEXIT Foundation.Please read to the end of this email (I have an important message at the end you don 't want to miss). When I founded BLEXIT, I wanted to make sure above all else we were NOT just a "think tank", but rather we pride ourselves on being a thoughtful and quick acting "DO-tank". We're doing things no other organization in the country is doing. Here are the BLEXIT Foundation's values, what we're doing to reach Black communities, and what we're fighting for: School Choice: Often, the people allegedly "fighting for minorities" are doing everything in their power to keep Black and Hispanic students locked into awful schools. The ugly truth is that these schools are simply brainwashing centers attacking the nuclear family, creating victims, and teaching their twisted values. BLEXIT is fighting for School Choice to empower young Black and Hispanic students to learn real skills, have more opportunities, and escape poverty. Criminal Justice Reform: The mainstream media wants urban communities to hate our law enforcement officers. This ugly message is setting Black Americans back. The BLEXIT Foundation supports our men and women who serve as police officers and defend our communities. Yes, we do need criminal justice reform. But we believe cooperating with our police officers and connecting as a community is critical to creating justice. BLEXIT Foundation is starting conversations with police officers and urban communities. The media wants you to believe cops are evil! This is wrong. But people never hear this message. Entrepreneurship: I am passionate about empowering Black and Hispanic Americans. Free markets, responsibility, and advancing the careers of urban communities is the solution to poverty. The BLEXIT Foundation identifies young leaders in urban communities and helps them create resumes, practice interviews, and learn skills employers desire. BLEXIT is not a book club or simply talking a big game... No, we're actually making an impact with real individuals committed to serving urban communities. Teaching History: Critical Race Theory has nothing to do with solving race relations in our country. This is about division and spreading a victimhood message. The BLEXIT Foundation believes in teaching real American history. It is critical for our urban communities to learn about our Founding Fathers and the Constitution to become productive and educated citizens. Presenting Our Message: **We have failed to reach urban communities with our message and values. Instead, we've allowed the mainstream media to write the narrative. Not anymore. The BLEXIT Foundation is going places where no one else goes. We're hosting events in Detroit, Baltimore, and Tampa and starting real conversations. We're not just preaching to the choir, and these aren't boring events. They have music, art, and real conversations, and they are fun! I am committed to reaching urban communities and spreading our message. What does "BLEXIT" mean? BLEXIT is the Black exit from the culture of victimhood, fear, and dependence the media has forced on us. If you hold a permanent view of yourself as a victim you become your own oppressor. This is personal to me. I was once a slave to a culture of victimhood and became a proud Black patriot. Friend, I am asking you to join the BLEXIT Foundation by making an investment in our organization. Please use one of the links I've copied below to donate today: This is my last message to you before you close this email. The BLEXIT Foundation believes in faith, family, and freedom. At the end of the day, our message to urban communities is about love and empowerment. My burning desire is to use my voice and change the direction of our urban communities and the country. The BLEXIT Foundation has a real impact, and I am inviting you to join our cause. Thank you, Friend. Candace Owens President BLEXIT Foundation Conservative Liberty Wire 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 | _

name: Karen Greenhalgh
subject: The Greenhalgh Gazette - April 15, 2023
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Karen Greenhalgh for Delegate
body:
Legislative News from the Office of Delegate Karen Greenhalgh
| | |
---
**Saturday, April 15th, 2023** Volume II, Issue No. 5
---
| | **R ECONVENED SESSION ROUND-UP
**
---
| | This week, my fellow Delegates and Senators came back to the Capitol for Reconvened Session, informally known as "Veto Session". We reviewed Governor Youngkin's vetoes and changes he recommended to the bills passed by the General Assembly in January and February. In total, of the 819 enrolled bills communicated to the Governor's Office, he signed 738, or approximately 90%. Of the remaining 10%, three were vetoed and 77 had recommended amendments for our consideration. For vetoed bills, one was from the House of Delegates and two were from the Senate. For bills returned with amendments, 38 were from House bills and 39 from the Senate. Notable highlights include identical companion bills HB2024/SB1310, HB1943/SB989, HB2325/SB1438, and stand-alone HB1929. HB2024 and SB1310 were carried by Delegate Leftwich (R-78) in the House and Senator Deeds in the Senate (D-25). As enrolled by the legislature, they would amend the list of people for which using or publishing their identity to coerce, intimidate, or harass carries an enhanced charge. Judges, justices, and magistrates would be included. The Governor's recommended amendment included an additional provision to create a misdemeanor offense from picketing or parading in or near a residence occupied by a judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee with the intent of intimidating or influencing the discharge of their duties, or obstructing administration of justice. In a party-line vote, the Senate rejected the amendments, returning the bills to the Governor, unchanged. He has three options now: approve the bills as originally passed, veto them, or take no action. If the May 12th action deadline passes without a decision, the bills are automatically enacted. Carried by Delegate Runion (R-25) and Senator Peake (R-22), HB2325 and SB1438 would prohibit foreign adversaries from acquiring agricultural land in Virginia. Proposed amendments offer clarifications such as permitting the state to hold a vested interest in land subject to a prohibited transaction, allowing subsequent non-adversary purchasers to retain title, and expressly grants rights to file certain actions in the local circuit court (to the land under dispute) by non-adversary third-parties involved in a prohibited transaction. Lastly, foreign adversary would be prevented from seeking restitution from state invention. The Governor's amendments were adopted with partial bipartisan support. Notably, some legislators switched their stance on the companion bills crossed over from the opposite chamber. Senators Bagby, Barker, Howell, and Morrissey switched from Nay to Yea, voting to concur with the Governor on HB2325. Conversely, Delegate Murphy switched her original vote of Yea to Nay after SB1438 crossed over to the House. Delegate Tara Durant's (R-28) HB1929, which has no Senate counterpart, extends the Interstate Compact on Educational Opportunity for Military Children (ICEOMC) to cover school-age dependents of foreign service employees and civilian members of the U.S. Armed Forces. The ICEOMC facilitates student transition for children of military families through member states implementing uniform standards for issuance and acceptance of student records. All 50 states have adopted the ICEOMC. Virginia was among the first, with former Delegate Mark Cole (R-88) carrying the bill to join the Compact in 2009. Amendments proposed by the Governor would guarantee certain rights and privileges to the newly covered students that are congruent with those to whom the ICEOMC already applies. In unanimous votes from the House and Senate, Governor Youngkin's recommendations were adopted. For all of the bills we considered during Reconvened Session, complete details are available at the following links for [vetoed bills](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/986b04d9ab111c0e/820aa9f4a7b16615c3d6b838967d376c/37923) as well as [bills with recommendations.](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/986b04d9ab111c0e/3bb079940ef64299c3d6b838967d376c/37923)
---
| | **V IRGINIA NATIONAL GUARD SOLDIERS HONORED FOR FEDERAL SERVICE
**
---
| |
---
| | It was a privilege to participate in presenting the Governor's National Service Medal (GNSM) to the invaluable soldiers serving the Commonwealth in the Virginia National Guard. One of the most rewarding parts of my job as a legislator is having the opportunity to acknowledge the sacrifices and risks that military personnel make for our communities and our nation every day. Created in 2005 by Governor Mark Warner, the GNSM recognizes Virginia Army and Air National Guard service members who have been called to active federal duty and subsequently demobilized. All enlisted men and women of the VNG returning from federal service, beginning on September 11, 2001, are eligible for the award.
---
| | The recipient soldiers are all assigned to the 576th Engineer Utilities Detachment, 276th Engineer Battalion, and 329th Regional Support Group, all based in Virginia Beach. They supported U.S. Central Command’s Operation Spartan Shield from May 2022 to February 2023. Deployed to Iraq, Syria, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait, their objective was completion of major physical security improvement projects including traffic control points, gates, bunkers and embankments. Representing Congresswoman Jen Kiggans was Nolan Bursch, who helms her local district office. With the soldiers' families in attendance, Col. Todd Hubbard, Chief of Staff of the Virginia Army National Guard, awarded the medals, each bearing the Great Seal of Virginia on its obverse. Mr. Bursch and myself joined the 276th's commanding officers, Lt. Col. Fleishman and Sgt. Maj. Coleman in individually congratulating each guardsman and guardswoman.

---
|
---
| | **V IRGINIA BEACH HERO SAVES FOUR FROM DROWNING
**
---
| |
---
On Thursday, March 23rd, a car plunged into a small, but deceptively deep pond in the Carolanne Farms neighborhood just off South Witchduck Road. Due to a driver miscalculation, the car and its four occupants found themselves trapped and rapidly sinking. Upon being alerted of the waterbourne vehicle by a neighbor, Sandy Wilson rushed from his home adjacent to the pond to intervene. Discovering the doors would not budge, he resorted to pulling the passengers up and out through the vehicle's sunroof. Thanks to his swift action, all four people survived the incident; the worst injury sustained was getting thoroughly drenched in pond water. When word of Sandy's heroic feat was brought to my attention, it didn't take long to decide that a commendation was warranted. In the week following the pond fiasco, Governor Youngkin was already scheduled to be in the area. Knowing he'd share my enthusiasm, I coordinated with his Office to set up something special for Sandy. Meeting up at the Brickhouse Diner on Providence Road, Governor Youngkin and I presented Sandy with an official certificate of gubernatorial recognition for exceptional service to the Commonwealth. Having gotten acquainted with Sandy Wilson, I know him to be very modest, so I will indulge that modesty momentarily. Good people like Sandy Wilson are everywhere. His reflexive willingness to help his fellow man isn't especially unique. On countless occasions, every day, people selflessly help strangers in pivotal moments of urgent need, thus making their communities tangibly better places to live. Such routine acts of goodness overwhelmingly receive no fanfare or expression of gratitude. On these rare occasions when exceptional bravery, sacrifice, or charity are drawn into the public consciousness, it presents an opportunity to applaud not only one person, but also to celebrate the natural, inherent human instinct to look out for each other.
---
| | **H AMPTON UNIVERSITY INAUGURATES NEW PRESIDENT
**
---
| |
---
On April 1st, Hampton University formally inaugurated its latest President. Assuming leadership of the the historically-black university in 2022, Darrell K. Williams is a graduate of Hampton University and a retired U.S. Army officer, holding the rank of Lieutenant General. From 2015-2017, he directed the Combined Arms Support Command (CASCOM), headquartered at Fort Lee, Virginia. CASCOM is one of the Army's major training and education divisions, administering the Ordnance, Quartermaster and Transportation schools, the Soldier Support Institute and the Army Logistics University. Following retirement from active duty military status, President Williams transitioned to the private sector. As a Vice President at Leidos, a Virginia-based IT firm and defense contractor, he managed the company's most high-stakes military accounts prior to beginning his tenure at Hampton University.
I had the great pleasure of meeting with President Williams at the post-inauguration reception. As a member of the House Subcommittee on Higher Education, I look forward to hearing his perspective and input on bills brought before the subcommittee in next year's legislative session.

**Me****n of Faith** For their annual visit to the state and federal Capitols, Men of Faith's President, Pastor Ron Taylor led a group of nearly 50 young men to see in the Senate and House of Delegates in session. Men of Faith's mission is the empowerment, encouragement, and advancement of young, African-American men in Virginia Beach's public school system. Pastor Taylor also serves as the President of the Hampton Roads Black Caucus. At the Caucus's April meeting, I presented him with a transcribed copy of my floor introduction of the Men of Faith from February 23rd.

| **VB Amateur Radio Club** An issue brought to my attention by the Virginia Beach Amateur Radio Club was the inability of members from clubs across Virginia to use flashing amber lights on personal vehicles while actively assisting law enforcement. Amatuer radio groups routinely coordinate with police on mobile radio operations during high-traffic events and emergencies. I carried HB1375 to permit their flashing light exemption. After failing twice before in 2016 and 2017, the VBARC were instrumental in the bill's success this session, contributing testimony to the the Transportation Committees in the House and Senate. To express my gratitude their volunteer services to the City of Virginia Beach, I introduced a House Joint Resolution to commend their work. Senators DeSteph and Cosgrove carried the resolution in the Senate. During their monthly membership meeting, I delivered the VBARC a presentation copy of their resolution.

# **VA Dept. of Education Seeking Vendors for Learning Recovery Grant Program** __
---
As of April 11th, applications to provide tutoring services in the Learning Recovery Grant (LRG) Program may be submitted. LRG's are an upcoming program of the Virginia Department of Education. Once launched, parents may apply for grants through an online portal (currently in development). Parents of students awarded grants will select vetted, pre-approved tutoring service providers. Grant payments will be made directly to registered providers. A total of $30 million has been reserved for the program, with each standard grant assigned $1,500. For students of families with incomes at 300% or less of the federal poverty level, grants will provide $3,000 in services. Eligibility requires recipients to be public, private, parochial or home-school students, residing in Virginia, who meet the legal definition of "school age". Once launched, LRGs will fund tutoring of qualifying K-12 students in English, mathematics, science, history/social studies, and foreign languages needed for diploma requirements. Grants may also pay for specialized services, such as speech and language therapy; and specialized equipment, such as assistive technology and augmentative communication devices. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to be an LRG vendor, use the following link to find the [submission portal and additional information.](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/986b04d9ab111c0e/2e5b90a044aa8099c3d6b838967d376c/37923)
---

## _District Procurement Report_
---
| | Each year, the Virginia Department of General Services (DGS) delivers a procurement report to each legislator detailing the Commonwealth's procurement activity within each legislative district. The DGS is an administrative agency fulfilling functions including state-owned facility and fleet management, building and infrastructure engineering, graphic design and printing of official publications, and procurement of materials and equipment for state operations. In the 2022 fiscal year, the Commonwealth spent $9.9 billion at Virginian businesses, across 588,005 purchase orders. Within the current 85th District in Virginia Beach, DGS made a total of $255 million in purchases from 1,030 businesses. Of those, 271 are certified as [small, women, or minority owned (SWaM)](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/986b04d9ab111c0e/4fcd64eef7316272c3d6b838967d376c/37923). The top five commodities procured from District 85 were: 1\. Value Engineering / Value Analysis Services; 2\. Software Maintenance / Support; 3\. Engineering Consulting; 4\. Microcomputer Application Software; 5\. Database Software.
---
|
---
| | |
---
# _School Board Votes to Reinstate Valedictorian & Salutatorian Honors _
---
| | In a 10-1 vote late last month, the Virginia Beach School Board reinstated the titles of valedictorian and salutatorian for graduating high school students. The move is a reversal of the Board's decision to eliminate the honorifics almost exactly five years earlier in April of 2018. Beginning in the 2023-2024 school year, two students demonstrating the highest overall academic achievement will once again be designated valedictorian and salutatorian. The original rationale behind the 2018 decision was a perceived unfair advantage held by students in advanced academic programs available at certain Virginia Beach high schools, such as the Governor's STEM Academy at Landstown High School. Additional classes completed in these specialized programs can bolster a student's weighted grade point average. Because there are limited seats, there can theoretically be a high-performing student who is not selected for an advanced program, who will have to perform exceptionally well in standard courses to remain on par with AP students, effectively having to compensate for fewer total classes being available. Nevertheless, the designations for graduating classes will be returning next year. To read the full story, you can find the [Virginian-Pilot article here](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/986b04d9ab111c0e/011fa29593f26dfcc3d6b838967d376c/37923). _Caption: Princess Anne High School's Class of 2019 commencement exercises._
---

| | **Could YOU Determine Control of the House of Delegates?**
---
**By Guest Contributor****B RENT THORNTON**
---
| | You may remember in 2017, the winner of Virginia’s 94th House of Delegates district came down to a drawing of names. You read that correctly: Due to a tie in votes, the race, and by extension control of the House of Delegates, was decided by a candidate’s name being picked from a bowl. While this may seem like an archaic way for 21st-century Virginia to break a tie, the truth of the matter is, it was completely avoidable. How? If just one more person had shown up to vote for either candidate, a clear winner would have emerged. Unfortunately for off-year elections, voter turnout is generally lower than in the major contests. Part of this may be due to election fatigue—we see campaign ads almost year-round and as soon as one election is over, pundits are already talking about the next. Another reason may be a lack of familiarity with the candidates or issues, or even simply a lack of urgency. Whatever the reason, the importance of casting an informed vote in _every_ election does not diminish. In fact, voting in off-year elections becomes even more vital because it typically takes fewer votes to win. If that’s not convincing, the above mentioned vote margin was hardly an anomaly. In the 2019 General Assembly election, there were four House of Delegates races decided by less than 700 votes, including two that were decided by less than 200 votes. In the 2021 General Assembly election, there were six House of Delegates races decided by less than 700 votes. In two of those six races, including the race Delegate Greenhalgh won, the margin was less than 130 votes. _130 votes_. Most people have more social media friends than that! Those 130 people decided their vote mattered and showed up to vote, and it meant the difference between victory and defeat for those candidates. With a 52-48 split between Republicans and Democrats, it also determined control of the House of Delegates, and by extension, the direction of the Commonwealth. With these figures in mind, I want to encourage everyone who reads this to be sure to vote in the general election on November 7th. Vote in the primary on June 20th. Vote in person on election day, or vote early, or vote absentee. No matter how you vote, the important thing is that you vote. Persuade your family and friends to vote too! You never know, your efforts may determine who wins the race—or even who controls the House of Delegates.
---
_The above editorial was authored by Brent Thornton, the Bill Research Specialist for the_ _Office of Delegate Karen Greenhalgh from the 2022 Regular Session to the present._

clean_body: Legislative News from the Office of Delegate Karen Greenhalgh | | | Saturday, April 15th, 2023 Volume II, Issue No. 5 | | R ECONVENED SESSION ROUND-UP | | This week, my fellow Delegates and Senators came back to the Capitol for Reconvened Session, informally known as "Veto Session". We reviewed Governor Youngkin's vetoes and changes he recommended to the bills passed by the General Assembly in January and February. In total, of the 819 enrolled bills communicated to the Governor's Office, he signed 738, or approximately 90%. Of the remaining 10%, three were vetoed and 77 had recommended amendments for our consideration. For vetoed bills, one was from the House of Delegates and two were from the Senate. For bills returned with amendments, 38 were from House bills and 39 from the Senate. Notable highlights include identical companion bills HB2024/SB1310, HB1943/SB989, HB2325/SB1438, and stand-alone HB1929. HB2024 and SB1310 were carried by Delegate Leftwich (R-78) in the House and Senator Deeds in the Senate (D-25). As enrolled by the legislature, they would amend the list of people for which using or publishing their identity to coerce, intimidate, or harass carries an enhanced charge. Judges, justices, and magistrates would be included. The Governor's recommended amendment included an additional provision to create a misdemeanor offense from picketing or parading in or near a residence occupied by a judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee with the intent of intimidating or influencing the discharge of their duties, or obstructing administration of justice. In a party-line vote, the Senate rejected the amendments, returning the bills to the Governor, unchanged. He has three options now: approve the bills as originally passed, veto them, or take no action. If the May 12th action deadline passes without a decision, the bills are automatically enacted. Carried by Delegate Runion (R-25) and Senator Peake (R-22), HB2325 and SB1438 would prohibit foreign adversaries from acquiring agricultural land in Virginia. Proposed amendments offer clarifications such as permitting the state to hold a vested interest in land subject to a prohibited transaction, allowing subsequent non-adversary purchasers to retain title, and expressly grants rights to file certain actions in the local circuit court (to the land under dispute) by non-adversary third-parties involved in a prohibited transaction. Lastly, foreign adversary would be prevented from seeking restitution from state invention. The Governor's amendments were adopted with partial bipartisan support. Notably, some legislators switched their stance on the companion bills crossed over from the opposite chamber. Senators Bagby, Barker, Howell, and Morrissey switched from Nay to Yea, voting to concur with the Governor on HB2325. Conversely, Delegate Murphy switched her original vote of Yea to Nay after SB1438 crossed over to the House. Delegate Tara Durant's (R-28) HB1929, which has no Senate counterpart, extends the Interstate Compact on Educational Opportunity for Military Children (ICEOMC) to cover school-age dependents of foreign service employees and civilian members of the U.S. Armed Forces. The ICEOMC facilitates student transition for children of military families through member states implementing uniform standards for issuance and acceptance of student records. All 50 states have adopted the ICEOMC. Virginia was among the first, with former Delegate Mark Cole (R-88) carrying the bill to join the Compact in 2009. Amendments proposed by the Governor would guarantee certain rights and privileges to the newly covered students that are congruent with those to whom the ICEOMC already applies. In unanimous votes from the House and Senate, Governor Youngkin's recommendations were adopted. For all of the bills we considered during Reconvened Session, complete details are available at the following links for as well as | | V IRGINIA NATIONAL GUARD SOLDIERS HONORED FOR FEDERAL SERVICE | | | | It was a privilege to participate in presenting the Governor's National Service Medal (GNSM) to the invaluable soldiers serving the Commonwealth in the Virginia National Guard. One of the most rewarding parts of my job as a legislator is having the opportunity to acknowledge the sacrifices and risks that military personnel make for our communities and our nation every day. Created in 2005 by Governor Mark Warner, the GNSM recognizes Virginia Army and Air National Guard service members who have been called to active federal duty and subsequently demobilized. All enlisted men and women of the VNG returning from federal service, beginning on September 11, 2001, are eligible for the award. | | The recipient soldiers are all assigned to the 576th Engineer Utilities Detachment, 276th Engineer Battalion, and 329th Regional Support Group, all based in Virginia Beach. They supported U.S. Central Command’s Operation Spartan Shield from May 2022 to February 2023. Deployed to Iraq, Syria, Jordan, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait, their objective was completion of major physical security improvement projects including traffic control points, gates, bunkers and embankments. Representing Congresswoman Jen Kiggans was Nolan Bursch, who helms her local district office. With the soldiers' families in attendance, Col. Todd Hubbard, Chief of Staff of the Virginia Army National Guard, awarded the medals, each bearing the Great Seal of Virginia on its obverse. Mr. Bursch and myself joined the 276th's commanding officers, Lt. Col. Fleishman and Sgt. Maj. Coleman in individually congratulating each guardsman and guardswoman. | | | V IRGINIA BEACH HERO SAVES FOUR FROM DROWNING | | On Thursday, March 23rd, a car plunged into a small, but deceptively deep pond in the Carolanne Farms neighborhood just off South Witchduck Road. Due to a driver miscalculation, the car and its four occupants found themselves trapped and rapidly sinking. Upon being alerted of the waterbourne vehicle by a neighbor, Sandy Wilson rushed from his home adjacent to the pond to intervene. Discovering the doors would not budge, he resorted to pulling the passengers up and out through the vehicle's sunroof. Thanks to his swift action, all four people survived the incident; the worst injury sustained was getting thoroughly drenched in pond water. When word of Sandy's heroic feat was brought to my attention, it didn't take long to decide that a commendation was warranted. In the week following the pond fiasco, Governor Youngkin was already scheduled to be in the area. Knowing he'd share my enthusiasm, I coordinated with his Office to set up something special for Sandy. Meeting up at the Brickhouse Diner on Providence Road, Governor Youngkin and I presented Sandy with an official certificate of gubernatorial recognition for exceptional service to the Commonwealth. name: =?utf-8?Q?Douglas=20Carswell?=
subject: Easy money economics is a disaster – update from your favorite think tank
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/leadershipacademy-8132976?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
|
Dear Friend,

**My friend Liz Truss, the former British Prime Minister, was in America this week.**
---
|
---
_British Prime Minister in Washington._
| **Speaking at the Heritage Foundation, she warned that America, Britain and the wider West were in danger of “becoming social democracies by the backdoor.”** She is right. **In a free market economy, private capital is allocated by those that own it. That is why it is called ‘capitalism’.** The risks of investing private capital are meant to correlate with reward. In a capitalist system, it is these millions of individual investment decisions that direct economic activity. **Instead, what we have seen over the past couple of decadeshas been the emergence of a system of central bank-backed crony capitalism.** The US Federal Reserve has taken to conjuring up vast amounts of new money, and interest rates have been kept artificially low. According to the theory, this was supposed to stimulate economic growth. It did – for a while. **But the longer easy money economics keeps going the more obvious it is that it has created ghost growth, not real prosperity. ** Christopher Leonard’s brilliant new book, The Lords of Easy Money, which I have been reading shows how easy money economics has corrupted the free market system on both sides of the Atlantic.
---
|
---
| **Artificially low interest rates and printing money have all kinds of secondary consequences. ** Those who own assets, or who work for some of those banks and venture funds privileged to be recipients of the easy money, become fabulously rich. Investments are made in things that they should not be. Corporate debt balloons. Economic activity is stimulated were it should not be. Over time, growth and productivity stall. This is pretty much precisely what has happened in the US and the UK. Ironically in Britain, when Prime Minister Truss attempted introduce the kind of free market reforms that might just pull the country out of this mess, bad investments made by UK insurance firms threatened a full scale financial crisis. **Truss was forced out of office after only 49 days.** Tragically for the UK, the established economic orthodoxy that got the country into this mess is now as entrenched as ever. Back in the 1960s and 70s, economic ‘experts’ came to believe that they could engineer economic growth in the UK and the US by what they called fiscal stimulus. By essentially spending more money, the economic establishment believed the government could make us all rich. Today a new set of ‘experts’ believe that they can generate wealth not with fiscal stimulus, but by manipulating the money supply. Because they are managing interest rates, rather than setting taxes and budgets, they face even less accountability for doing so. **Monetary stimulus is no more effective at producing permanent prosperity than fiscal stimulus proved to be. ** In the late 1970s, conservatives figured out an alternative to the Keynesian fiscal orthodoxies. Conservatives in America today need to figure out an alternative to easy money economics – and fast. **Monetary policy today is corrupting the free market system. If we are not careful it will lead to the permanent socialization of the American economy.** Have a wonderful weekend!
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=ee2e40d429&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| | |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO

---
|
---
| | _ _

**Our mailing address is:**
Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013

Friend,
I sat down with Tucker Carlson on Tuesday night for my FIRST INTERVIEW since I was arrested and arraigned.
_Tucker asked if there 's **ANYTHING** the Democrats could throw at me that would make me drop out of the presidential race._
Without hesitation, I said, _**" NO. I'D NEVER DROP OUT."**_
Because the fate of America hangs in the balance - and I will _NEVER SURRENDER_ our country to the radical Left.
If an unjust, and possibly illegal, ARREST didn't even make me flinch - then I promise you, NOTHING WILL.
**Now, I 'm asking YOU to stand with me as the Left tries to throw everything they can at us to _force me out of the presidential election._**

name: Jon Tester
subject: I hate to bother you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| Folks,

I hate to bother you, but this is really important.

My finance team just called to update me on where things stand with our critical mid-month goal coming up.

**Here’s the bad news: they told me we’re still about $25,000 short. Early deadlines like this can make or break a campaign, so it’s critical we report the strongest numbers possible.**

The GOP has already launched early attacks against me so this is the worst time to fall behind.

Asking for money is my least favorite part of the job, but we’ve got to close the gap between now and midnight.

[**I’ll get to the point: Can you split $10 (or more!) between my campaign and Voter Protection Project to help us get back on track before our mid-month deadline? I’d really appreciate it.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/43575?t=6&akid=7114%2E878179%2ElmeAfW) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Jon Tester and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/43576?t=7&akid=7114%2E878179%2ElmeAfW)

clean_body: | Folks, I hate to bother you, but this is really important. My finance team just called to update me on where things stand with our critical mid-month goal coming up. Here’s the bad news: they told me we’re still about $25,000 short. Early deadlines like this can make or break a campaign, so it’s critical we report the strongest numbers possible. The GOP has already launched early attacks against me so this is the worst time to fall behind. Asking for money is my least favorite part of the job, but we’ve got to close the gap between now and midnight. I am proud to fight for the values that matter most to our communities: affordable healthcare, quality education, a clean environment, and good-paying jobs. But I can’t do it alone.

That's why I'm reaching out to you today. We’re working to hit our mid-month fundraising goal, and I am asking for your support. Your donation will help us continue to spread our message of progress across our district and help us retake the House.

**[So I’m asking: Can I count on you to make a donation of any amount to support our campaign?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61828970/401340709/-818894537?refcode=EM_FR_230415FR_N_MK_90DA_4_NG_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjlhZjJjYi04ZmRiLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cam4pIY1PANcuPngvRD9YTddyD0V4CoVdrvGRMcrPLA=)** We’re going to be up against Kevin McCarthy’s entire political machine, and we need to be ready to fight back.

**You can use this personal link to give:** **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kaptur_april2023](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/61828973/401340711/-818894537?refcode=EM_FR_230415FR_N_MK_90DA_4_NG_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY1NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NjlhZjJjYi04ZmRiLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cam4pIY1PANcuPngvRD9YTddyD0V4CoVdrvGRMcrPLA=)**

Together, we can build a better future for all Ohioans. With your support, we can continue to fight for the values that matter most.

Thank you,

clean_body: I’m asking you to join me today. | | = I am proud to fight for the values that matter most to our communities: affordable healthcare, quality education, a clean environment, and good-paying jobs. But I can’t do it alone. That's why I'm reaching out to you today. We’re working to hit our mid-month fundraising goal, and I am asking for your support. Your donation will help us continue to spread our message of progress across our district and help us retake the House. = We’re going to be up against Kevin McCarthy’s entire political machine, and we need to be ready to fight back. You can use this personal link to give: = Together, we can build a better future for all Ohioans. With your support, we can continue to fight for the values that matter most. Thank you, Marcy Kaptur | | Congresswoman Marcy Kaptur is a champion for the Midwest in Congress. She is focused on getting big money out of politics, holding Wall Street accountable, fighting against bad trade deals that harm workers, ensuring access to health care for all, and restoring our Great Lakes. We’re not sure why, but it seems like _everything_ is falling apart right now in the United States, what with **Black lawmakers being subjected to racially-motivated expulsions, Trump-appointed judges interfering in the FDA approval of abortion pills, and Supreme Court Justices accepting luxury gifts from Nazi-sympathizing GOP megadonors.**

And on top of all of that? **_Our fundraising totals have taken a huge hit_ — right when we desperately need to be at the top of our game.**

[We know we ask a lot of you, Peter, but the work we do isn’t cheap. Times like these are when our country needs Black leaders most, and we’re determined to power as many Black candidates to victory as possible to help us right the course of this country. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. And on top of all of that? Our fundraising totals have taken a huge hit — right when we desperately need to be at the top of our game. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately. | | | | | | — The Collective PAC The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. name: Jon Tester
subject: It’s planting season
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-tester [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-tester]
Folks,
It’s planting season and Sharla and I have been waiting for the snow to melt and the water to recede from some of our fields. In the meantime, we’re getting the equipment ready. You can plan as much as you want, but Mother Nature calls the shots.
I gotta tell ya, I love this time of year — sure, it is a bit stressful with all the work we have to get done. But getting my hands in the soil and running the tractor for 16 hours a day really grounds me. With some hard work and a little luck, we will set ourselves up for a good harvest.
In fact, the work Sharla and I do on the farm is kind of like the campaign we’re running. Fundraising early helps plant the seeds of our campaign so that we can continue to grow our movement and, ultimately, come home with a successful harvest on Election Day.
So, here’s the part where I ask: Can I count on you to help us *sow the seeds* of our campaign by splitting a donation between me and Seth Moulton to help us reach our critical April mid-month goal? I’d really appreciate it. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-tester]
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-tester]
Farming allows me to take time to work in the open and quiet fields. It reminds me what’s important, and reaffirms my commitment to representing Montana. I’m not in this race to get rich. I’m running because I’m not done fighting for you and your family.
National Republicans will spend tens of millions in shady dark money to try and stop us.
But just like in planting, you reap what you sow. That’s why I care so much about building this campaign up from the grassroots.
OK — I’m going to get back to the shop to work on my tractor, thanks for your support!
Jon
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-tester]
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/r_WYy1BSbkmUjvconTI5TQ2?unsubscribedata=K0t71ZUAX3YR09xBrmYmbxHZKTXWmqm9ZdUnU05lqiabOWvSY5Gg6NrhAklBJBnTAi3pAoobcgXhzgLnT7H3ZpaZ0B8xaE%2FvIy4ECvHWDvH%2Fl6j7iUkopqgCxyuphZ%2F%2BEZ%2FpYf%2BJGObckl8oQ4zecXlO8kXh4Q88zCAXrgUc5gZ9IxhhNbmmmtodKIvAQHZBOD1UeZxlE30%2BmvEQiPbJCg9c4Xt7ZjGExtHprPRh7OCzZNBspcms0XpvzoHLZdJb3JyR1gi5uSJELmvo8gUPFOGrMxLI7nUjdt1XkdiqRExSiAQETgrYZtm0kp1oiNrL14GfinLUXaNtPuyIqBWVJnSlbHF%2FBg50cSLGwyhDFqX3YYIoGhN3j88TxLRwArbcE2I%2F3NeapdOMMWYRWRrtIJq6bEz%2FJi7gM6j0woxPL7g%3D .

name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
subject: my grandmother
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for CongressLisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apr2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel_logo [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apr2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel_logo] https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apri?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel_logo [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apri?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel_logo]Hi there,
I’m a big believer in role models. I’m fortunate to have many.
As a little girl, one of the people I looked up to was my grandmother — my mother’s mother. She worked at a ball-bearing plant as a steelworker at a time when there were very few women in that position, and she went on to become a union leader. Like any mom, she was concerned about keeping her neighborhood safe for her children and fought against the influx of drugs and guns where she lived. She may not have thought of herself as an activist, but in my mind she certainly was one. Her leadership and fearlessness is something I think about often.
As a young person, I was lucky that my first bosses were strong women who guided me and pushed me. They gave me confidence in myself and reinforced the same values my family instilled: working hard, leveling the playing field, and extending a hand to those who need it. Now, I take those values with me every day in Congress and in my personal life.
While there is reason to look at today’s role models for young people with skepticism, there is also reason for hope.
Today’s Congress has a record number of women. The Democratic Party has our first person of color serving as House Minority Leader.
But the reality is that right now, there are no Black women in the Senate. And the House has a long way to go before it looks like the people it serves. Women are still a long way from making up 50% of elected officeholders, and the LGBTQ+ community is underrepresented in American politics.
I know how important it is to have role models who look like you, and I know how much representation matters. That’s why I’ll always be an advocate for inclusion.
If that’s something that resonates with you, I hope that you’ll consider making a donation to my campaign — $10 or whatever you can — to help me continue that fight. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apr2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel]
Thanks for listening,
Lisa
Congresswoman Lisa Blunt Rochester is a Democrat representing the First State, Delaware. She served as the National Co-Chair of the Biden-Harris campaign and Co-Chair of the DCCC ‘Red to Blue’ program. Lisa is running for reelection to continue fighting for our health care, leading the charge toward environmental justice, and advocating for working families like hers.
DONATE MONTHLY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lbr-em-apr2023?refcode=EM_FR_20230415_rolemodel_bio&refcode2=biobox&recurring=1]
Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for CongressLisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
PO BOX 9767
Wilmington, DE 19810
United States

subject: Marjorie Taylor Greene is a dangerous traitor and we need to help Stacey Abrams as she turns Greene into an albatross around the national GOP’s neck
body:
Stacey Abrams is still fighting for all of us!
| | | | |

| | |

| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6fe8a8f7-d34f-4ed8-948b-da8ab72764e5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| | | | | Peter, _We all know Marjorie Taylor Greene, the racist, anti-Semitic, anti-gay, ignorant, cruel & incompetent loudmouth is unfit to be in Congress. _**_Yet, she just proved again, and I don't say this lightly: She. Is. A. Dangerous. Traitor._** **_The FBI arrested Justin Teixeira, a white supremacist who burrowed his way into the Air National Guard, stole and shared sensitive, classified secrets online about the war in Ukraine. It helped seditionists and genocidal madman Putin, while endangering Americans around the globe._ ** **_The utterly repulsive Greene immediately defended this scumbag traitor_** , _tweeting that President Biden was persecuting Teixeira because he's "white, male, Christian" and pro-Russia. Here's former Rep. Liz Cheney's response:_ [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcd4749df-d478-4ce0-9947-d288de8d1b47/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Former CIA Director John Brennan called her a dangerous security risk, unfit to be in the House. We must remove her._ **_Sadly, Greene's from a gerrymandered, pro-Trump district. Yet, we have Stacey Abrams. She already proved she can change the tide of history, when her group, Fair Fight, did such an amazing job registering and turning out Georgia voters that President Biden won there in a shocker, as did Senators Warnock (twice) & Ossoff. _** [**_Fair Fight's still changing political gravity in Georgia, and making Marjorie Taylor Greene the face of the GOP nationwide, an albatross. Will you join us in taking that first step towards removing Greene from office by giving $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to Stacey Abrams' Fair Fight?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta82c2f5d-a3db-45b1-aaa8-fd31ee765385/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Thank you for fighting for a Democratic America and against treason._ Cliff
